title: Restore the documented MMIO command barrier sequence
prompt:
The command submission path in `src/mmio_command.c` performs all of the required register accesses and barriers, but one operation is out of order. On targets with posted MMIO writes, the current sequence can issue the command-register readback before the write barrier has made the preceding argument and command writes observable. Repair the ordering at its root.

The device protocol is exact: write the 32-bit argument register, write the 16-bit command register, execute the bus write barrier, read back that same 16-bit command register to flush posted writes, execute the bus read barrier, and only then poll the 8-bit status register. Preserve those register widths and offsets. The readback value is intentionally ignored. A status read with the fault bit set returns the fault result (even if ready is also set); otherwise the ready bit returns success. The poll limit is the maximum number of status reads, so exhaustion returns timeout without an extra read, and a zero limit still submits and flushes the command before returning timeout.

Keep the injected MMIO access table and public result contract unchanged so the fake-register fixture can record every access. Do not replace accessor calls with direct register dereferences, add volatile qualifiers to the bus, access table, or command-path locals, cache or coalesce register accesses, add retries beyond the supplied poll limit, or modify the protected header or tests. Volatile qualification belongs in target-specific low-level accessors, outside this command path. Make the focused correction in `src/mmio_command.c`. The provided test command is offline and deterministic; do not weaken its strict C17 compiler flags.
verify_cmd: env PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py' -v

full_distill.primary_skill: repair a posted-MMIO write-barrier/readback/read-barrier sequence while preserving exact-width register access and bounded polling
full_distill.baseline_evidence: the strict C17 build succeeds, but the deterministic fake-register fixture exits 1 because its access log records the 16-bit command readback before the write barrier instead of after it
full_distill.success_contract: the fake bus observes argument write, command write, write barrier, command readback, read barrier, then bounded status reads; all offsets and widths remain exact; fault, ready, timeout, zero-budget, and invalid-interface behavior remain unchanged
adversarial_factors: the fake register bank records accessor width, offset, value, and barrier position, so merely retaining all operations in the wrong order cannot pass
success occurs after multiple scripted status reads and fault takes priority over ready, pinning existing status semantics
timeout checks require exactly the supplied number of 8-bit status reads, while a zero budget still requires the complete submission and flush prefix
adjacent sentinel fields and exact-width callback signatures expose widened command or status accesses
protected source-contract checks reject direct register dereferences, broad volatile qualification, weakened warning flags, and edits to the injected access-table API
